text: TikTok's terms frame the service as a highly moderated entertainment platform with aggressive discretion over content, accounts, recommendations, and features. Users allow TikTok to collect device data, usage patterns, identifiers, and signals related to how content is viewed, shared, or skipped. Uploaded videos, audio, and related materials are typically covered by a broad license that supports distribution, promotion, derivative formatting, and platform operations. The service can suspend accounts, limit visibility, or remove content based on policy enforcement or safety judgments. Terms updates may apply through continued use, and disputes are often routed through arbitration and class-action limits. Liability disclaimers and damages limitations further narrow the user's leverage.

cachedSummary.verdict: TikTok keeps broad rights over your content, visibility, and dispute options.

agreementCards.label: Content license
agreementCards.detail: They get wide rights to use, distribute, and adapt uploaded media.

agreementCards.label: Enforcement control
agreementCards.detail: Reach, removals, and suspensions can happen under broad moderation standards.

agreementCards.label: Data use
agreementCards.detail: Viewing behavior can be modeled for recommendations, ads, and product tuning.

agreementCards.label: Limited recourse
agreementCards.detail: Disputes may be pushed into arbitration instead of open court.

redFlags.title: Algorithmic visibility without clear appeal
redFlags.clause: We may remove or restrict access to content or accounts at our discretion and in line with our policies...
redFlags.whyItMatters: This is the practical power clause: the platform can hurt reach or access before you have a meaningful way to respond.

redFlags.title: Broad media license
redFlags.clause: You grant us and our affiliates a worldwide, royalty-free license to use, reproduce, adapt, publish and transmit your User Content...
redFlags.whyItMatters: If you are posting original work, this is the part that hands TikTok a lot of room to use it.

redFlags.title: Arbitration and class waiver
redFlags.clause: Any dispute will be resolved by binding arbitration and you waive any right to participate in a class action.
redFlags.whyItMatters: This makes it harder to fight a platform-wide problem together with other users.
